rows.description: Computus Thomas Stinton Coll. Exon. Rectoris a 2do Novr is 1795 ad eundem diem revolution 1796
rows.notes: Top-of-page title in Latin script. Handwriting mostly legible but several abbreviations (Novr, ad eundem) and flourishes make a fully 'crystal-clear' rating inappropriate.
Section heading below title. The word appears to be Latin ('Arrearagia recepta' / 'Arrearagia recep ta'); legible but slightly stylised.
Clear description and well-aligned amount in the three right-hand columns: 152 pounds, 17 shillings, 0 pence. Vertical ruling alignment unambiguous.

rows.description: Gwinear Rectory half a year to Nic'
rows.notes: Amount clearly '30 0 0'. Description reads 'Gwinear Rectory half a year to Nic'' — 'Gwinear' and terminal abbreviation Nic' inferred from handwriting; slight ambiguity in place name spelling.

Amount reads '17 . 2 . 10½' — the pence has a clear halfpenny loop ('½' / 'ob') interpreted as 0.5. Place name 'Riddington' / 'Riddington Mills' legible enough to transcribe though initial letter has flourish.
Amount '8 11 6' clearly aligned. Description 'Garsington Sadler' is readable and matches layout; very little ambiguity.
Second Garsington line with identical amount '8 11 6'. Handwriting clear and columns well-aligned.
Amount '41 13 10' is legible and in range. Place name (Tintinhul) is somewhat irregular in spelling but the entry and years are clear.
Amount appears as '5 1 11½' — pence shows a halfpenny loop interpreted as 0.5. Surname 'Beaton' reading is probable though slightly stylised.
Amount '2 0 0' is clear. The description looks like 'Montacute Alexr Smiths' (abbreviated Alexander) — some uncertainty in exact orthography of names.
Amount '7 16 2' aligns under the columns and is clear. 'Clifton Finny' is legible in the description.

rows.description: South Newington Tenement Tho. Penn 1 year
rows.notes: Amount '2 11 3' clearly visible. Description includes abbreviation 'Tho.' for Thomas and surname 'Penn' — handwriting is crisp here.
Amount '40 0 0' clear. Place/entry reads 'Menherst' (or similar variant) — the exact spelling of the place is faintly ambiguous but context (Rectory) is certain.
Amount '11 14 8' is well-formed and readable. 'Chace Hill' description is clear.
Amount '12 2 6' readable. The names 'Barrett and Burgess' appear after 'Bennington' but have slight flourishes; transcription chosen to match visible letters.
Amount '9 0 0' is visible, but the place name 'Tuthwyn' (or variant) is ambiguous from handwriting and the 'a year and 1/2' phrase interpretation is moderately uncertain.
Amount appears as '8 13 4' but spacing is tight near a drawn brace; the surname 'Comyn' inferred from letters that are somewhat indistinct. Column alignment plausible but moderately uncertain.
Amount '13 6 8' clear. 'Lad Lane Wilson' readable with reasonable clarity.

rows.description: Lad Lane Hallifax late Worsel 2 years
rows.notes: Amount '3 6 8' visible. Proper names 'Hallifax' and 'Worsel' are uncertain readings of stylised script; chosen letters match major loops but alternative spellings plausible.

A small bracketed subtotal is written as '£33 6 8' near the Lad Lane group. Numerals are legible but placement (a marginal subtotal) makes exact association with which subset it sums moderately uncertain.

A separate marginal number '17.13.4' is written at the right of the bracket — it appears to be a grouped subtotal but lacks an explicit label. Alignment makes its exact role ambiguous; numerals themselves are readable.
Amount '6 0 0' clear. 'Bampton 6 years' description is readable; letters are distinct.
Amount '6 10 10' clearly visible. Initials and surname 'S. G. Page Turner' are legible; spacing is tight but unambiguous.
Amount '0 10 0' (ten shillings) is clear and the institution 'Jesus College' is plainly written.
Amount '6 0 0' clear. Description 'Back Gate Coppins' readable; minor doubt on exact surname spelling but letters are distinct.
Amount '6 6 0' is readable. Description looks like 'Willd Arish' or similar; letters are less clear here so transcription of the proper name carries moderate uncertainty.
Final total at foot of column reads '392 3 5 1/4' — pence clearly written with a quarter-farthing/farthing mark (interpreted as 0.25). Numerals are clear; fraction symbol is distinct.
